title: Senecio steyermarkii

intro: Senecio steyermarkii, also known as Mexican fireweed, is a perennial plant species native to the high-altitude regions of Mexico and Central America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and bitter leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory issues, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Senecio steyermarkii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been attributed to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Senecio steyermarkii is commonly used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and colds.
